subject: Computer Networking
title: RTP: Streaming Media
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on RTP: Streaming Media.

hook.text: A video call needs audio and video packets in the right order at the right time. RTP handles that delivery.

buildup.text: RTP — Real-time Transport Protocol — adds timestamps and sequence numbers to media packets.

discovery.text: The receiver uses timestamps to play audio and video at the correct speed, even if packets arrive unevenly.

twist.text: RTP rides on UDP, not TCP. Retransmitting a late audio packet would cause worse delay than skipping it.

climax.text: Zoom, Teams, FaceTime — every major video platform relies on RTP to keep your calls smooth.

punchline.text: RTP keeps media in sync. Timing is everything.

quiz.question: What does RTP add to media packets?
